commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed for the PATH shell security decision and contributor-side real behavior proof; this should not be routed to an automated repair lane yet.; Needs attention: The patch touches shell execution and introduces a PATH-resolved shell fallback that needs security-boundary handling before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Avoid resolving the fallback shell through inherited PATH — src/infra/node-shell.ts:18 (sha=e10068237704990c94166d00da34ae85c730f586)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `/usr/bin/sh` and PATH-resolved `sh` retry candidates for node-host `system.run` after `/bin/sh` spawn ENOENT, plus focused unit coverage for the retry sequence.

Reproducibility: yes. The source path is clear: current main builds `/bin/sh -lc` for Linux node exec and returns spawn errors directly, and the linked issue provides concrete `exec host=node` commands with `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT` output.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed for the PATH shell security decision and contributor-side real behavior proof; this should not be routed to an automated repair lane yet.

target.title: Retry node shell fallback on ENOENT
target.body:
## Summary

- Retry node-host `system.run` shell execution with `/usr/bin/sh` and then PATH-resolved `sh` when the default `/bin/sh` spawn fails with ENOENT.
- Keep non-shell and Windows invocations unchanged.
- Add regression coverage for the runtime retry chain.

Fixes #85202

## Why

Linux headless node hosts can report `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT` even when the node is connected and `system.which`/manual host checks show a usable shell at `/usr/bin/sh`. The previous node-host path treated the first spawn error as terminal, so `exec host=node` failed before trying another POSIX shell path.

## Verification

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/node-host/invoke.node-shell-fallback.test.ts src/infra/node-shell.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.sanitize-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts`
  - 3 Vitest shards passed; 24 tests
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke.node-shell-fallback.test.ts`
  - 1 test passed

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Node-host `system.run` no longer treats a missing default `/bin/sh` spawn as the only possible POSIX shell path; the patched node host retries `/usr/bin/sh` and then PATH-resolved `sh` on ENOENT.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux VPS source checkout at `/root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback`, Node.js/Vitest executing the patched OpenClaw node-host runtime path with mocked spawn ENOENT failures followed by a real runtime assertion of the retry sequence.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke.node-shell-fallback.test.ts` from the patched checkout. The test imports the actual node-host `runCommand` implementation and simulates `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT`, then `spawn /usr/bin/sh ENOENT`, then a successful PATH `sh` execution.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the live command:
  ```text
   RUN  v4.1.7 /root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  1 passed (1)
     Start at  00:26:09
     Duration  2.49s (transform 2.32s, setup 1.22s, import 847ms, tests 216ms, environment 0ms)
  ```
  The passing test asserts the runtime called spawn in this order:
  ```text
  /bin/sh -lc "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"
  /usr/bin/sh -lc "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"
  sh -lc "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The node-host retry path continued through both ENOENT failures and returned success from PATH `sh` with stdout `NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK`.
- **What was not tested:** I do not have the reporter's exact Linux headless node/systemd setup, so I could not reproduce their original `/bin/sh` ENOENT on a connected remote node before patch. The PR keeps the fallback narrow and should still benefit from reporter or maintainer live validation on the exact #85202 environment.
target.author: clawsean
